title: Father Henson’s Story of His Own Life

memes.label: Abolition
memes.description: The movement to end chattel slavery in the British Empire and United States — a central theme of Henson's narrative and the political context of his life's work.

memes.label: Amos Lawrence
memes.description: Boston merchant and philanthropist who endorsed Josiah Henson's character, backed his business ventures, and facilitated his fundraising journeys to England.

memes.label: Amos Riley
memes.description: Brother of Isaac Riley, slaveholder in Kentucky to whom Josiah Henson was entrusted with fellow slaves in 1825.

memes.label: Auction Block
memes.description: The public sale of enslaved human beings on the auction block — a central mechanism of the slave economy and a defining trauma of Henson's childhood

memes.label: Bible
memes.description: The role of the Bible — as sacred text, object of reverence, and motivation for literacy — in the life and ministry of Josiah Henson.

memes.label: Boston
memes.description: Boston, Massachusetts — the city where Josiah Henson met with leading abolitionist philanthropists who funded the construction of a sawmill for the Dawn Settlement in Canada.

memes.label: Boston Mob Attack
memes.description: A mob attack on Josiah Henson in Boston during the Fugitive Slave Law era (c. 1851), followed by a public meeting where Henson defended himself and was vindicated — illustrating the danger faced by Black men in Northern cities even after the Compromise of 1850.

memes.label: British and Foreign Anti-Slavery Society
memes.description: The British and Foreign Anti-Slavery Society, whose secretary John Scobell served on the oversight committee for Josiah Henson's British fundraising during his 1851–1852 visit to England.

memes.label: British Committee of Twelve
memes.description: The committee of twelve prominent British figures — including abolitionists, clergymen, and businessmen — appointed in 1851 to oversee Josiah Henson's fundraising in England, with a sub-committee of three and a treasurer to manage all donated funds.

memes.label: Buffalo
memes.description: City on the eastern shore of Lake Erie in New York, the final American stop for Josiah Henson and his family before crossing to freedom in Canada

memes.label: Camp Meeting Conversion
memes.description: Josiah Henson's transformative religious conversion at a Methodist camp meeting, an event that shaped his moral character and vocation as a preacher.

memes.label: Canada
memes.description: Canada, the destination of Josiah Henson's escape from slavery in 1830 and the site of the Dawn Settlement for fugitive slaves.

memes.label: Charlotte Henson
memes.description: Josiah Henson's wife of over 40 years, who accompanied him on the escape to Canada and discovered the fraudulent freedom-purchase scheme in Kentucky.

memes.label: Childhood under Slavery
memes.description: The experience of childhood for enslaved children, as depicted in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Chillicothe
memes.description: Town in Ohio where the Ohio Conference of the Methodist Episcopal Church held its annual session, attended by Josiah Henson during his early ministry as a traveling exhorter and preacher.

memes.label: Christianity
memes.description: Central religious framework of the narrative, shaping Henson's moral outlook, preaching vocation, and understanding of freedom

relations.label: Ohio Conference of the Methodist Episcopal Church

memes.label: Cincinnati
memes.description: Cincinnati, the Ohio River city where Josiah Henson received critical aid from abolitionist allies during his preaching tour, his escape to freedom, and his Underground Railroad rescue missions.

memes.label: Confrontation with the Overseer
memes.description: Henson's violent confrontation with overseer Bryce Litton, resulting in a brutal beating that broke his arm and shoulder blades

memes.label: Convention of Blacks (London, 1838)
memes.description: The convention of Black delegates from settlements across Upper Canada, held in London in June 1838, which unanimously approved Josiah Henson's plan to establish a manual-labor school at the Dawn Settlement.

memes.label: Conversion Experience
memes.description: Josiah Henson's transformative religious experience during a voyage down the Mississippi River, when he nearly murdered four white men with an axe but was stopped by his Christian conscience — the pivotal spiritual turning point of his life.

memes.label: Dawn Settlement
memes.description: The Dawn Settlement near Dresden, Ontario, founded by Josiah Henson as a community for fugitive slaves, combining economic enterprise (lumbering) with educational opportunity through its Manual Labor School.

memes.label: Death of Charlotte Henson
memes.description: The death of Charlotte Henson — Josiah Henson's wife of over forty years — in 1852 at the Dawn Settlement, shortly after his return from a fundraising tour in England, ending a marriage forged in slavery and tested by escape and exile.

memes.label: Detroit
memes.description: Detroit, Michigan, the border city and free-territory destination on the Detroit River that Josiah Henson explored in 1834 as a site for Black settlement, and which served as a landmark on Underground Railroad escape routes toward Canada.

memes.label: Education
memes.description: The pursuit of education — literacy, schooling, and practical training — as a central theme in Josiah Henson's life and his efforts to uplift the fugitive slave community in Canada.

memes.label: England
memes.description: England, where Josiah Henson traveled in 1850–51 and 1852 for fundraising and abolitionist speaking, meeting with dignitaries including the Archbishop of Canterbury and Lord John Russell.

memes.label: Escape Route Network
memes.description: The network of locations and routes that Josiah Henson traveled during his escape from slavery in Kentucky to freedom in Canada, spanning multiple states and waterways through the Underground Railroad system.

memes.label: Escape to Canada
memes.description: Josiah Henson's dramatic escape from slavery in Kentucky to freedom in Canada with his wife Charlotte and their children, culminating in their arrival on October 28, 1830.

memes.label: Eusebius Smith
memes.description: British gentleman who served on Josiah Henson's oversight committee and sub-committee of three during the 1851 England fundraising visit.

memes.label: Exeter Hall
memes.description: Exeter Hall in London, a major venue for abolitionist and philanthropic meetings where Josiah Henson spoke about the condition of enslaved people during his 1850–51 visit to England.

memes.label: Faith and Hope
memes.description: Christian faith and hope as sustaining forces for enslaved people, as depicted in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Family Reunion at Dawn
memes.description: The emotional reunion of Josiah Henson with his wife Charlotte and their children after a desperate escape separated the family at the Ohio River crossing — a moment of profound relief and gratitude marking the successful conclusion of their flight from slavery.

memes.label: Family Separation
memes.description: The systematic breaking apart of enslaved families through sale, a central trauma of American slavery documented throughout Henson's narrative

memes.label: First Sermon
memes.description: Henson's entry into preaching after meeting a Methodist minister in Kentucky in 1828, leading to his first preaching tour and a new phase of his life

memes.label: Fort Erie
memes.description: Town on the Canadian shore of Lake Erie near Buffalo, site of a large religious meeting where Josiah Henson encountered a fugitive slave family

memes.label: Francis "Master Frank" Riley
memes.description: Brother of Isaac Riley's wife who established himself in business at Washington, D.C., served as Josiah Henson's ally and negotiator for his freedom purchase, and was the only witness to the true terms of the agreement.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: The pursuit and attainment of freedom from chattel slavery — the central driving force of Josiah Henson's life narrative and his ultimate achievement through escape to Canada in 1830.

memes.label: Fugitive Slave Law of 1850
memes.description: The 1850 U.S. federal law that required the return of escaped slaves and intensified the abolitionist movement

memes.label: Georgetown
memes.description: Georgetown, the Montgomery County settlement near the Riley plantation where John McKenny lived and where Josiah Henson first heard a Christian sermon, and one of the two markets where Henson sold his master's produce as plantation superintendent.

memes.label: H. Ingersoll Bowditch
memes.description: Boston physician and abolitionist who, along with Amos Lawrence and Samuel Eliot, endorsed Josiah Henson's bank note to complete the Dawn Settlement sawmill.

memes.label: Henson Family Tree
memes.description: A genealogical overview of the family of Josiah Henson, including his parents, wife Charlotte, children, siblings, and extended family members mentioned throughout his autobiographical narrative.

memes.label: Hiram Wilson
memes.description: Congregational missionary from Massachusetts who worked with Josiah Henson to improve conditions for Black settlers in Canada from 1836 onward.

memes.label: Indiana
memes.description: Free state bordering the Ohio River, traversed by Josiah Henson and other fugitive slaves on their journey northward to Canada, including the Quaker settlement at Richmond that provided crucial aid.

memes.label: Isaac Riley
memes.description: Isaac Riley, the Montgomery County, Maryland slaveholder who purchased Josiah Henson's mother and served as Henson's principal master during his youth and early adulthood.

memes.label: James C. Fuller
memes.description: English-born Quaker residing in Skeneateles, New York, who raised fifteen hundred dollars in England for the Canadian black settlements at the urging of Hiram Wilson.

memes.label: James Lightfoot
memes.description: Escaped slave whose plea for help at a Fort Erie meeting moved Josiah Henson to undertake a perilous rescue mission to bring his family to freedom in Canada.

memes.label: James Sherman
memes.description: London minister and abolitionist who hosted Josiah Henson's preaching in his pulpit and served on the committee overseeing his 1851 England fundraising.

memes.label: John Branch
memes.description: London clergyman who served on Josiah Henson's oversight committee and sub-committee during the 1851 England fundraising visit.

memes.label: John Lobb
memes.description: British lay preacher, businessman, and close ally of Josiah Henson who wrote the preface to the 1858 London edition of Henson's autobiography.

memes.label: John Rolfe
memes.description: Reverend John Rolfe of Toronto, who provided Josiah Henson with letters of introduction to England and presided over the mass meeting that vindicated Henson against false charges. (020-005)

memes.label: John Scobell
memes.description: Secretary of the British and Foreign Anti-Slavery Society who was sent as an agent to Canada to investigate charges against Josiah Henson and later cleared his name.

memes.label: Josiah Henson
memes.description: Josiah Henson (1789–1883), author of the 1858 narrative, formerly enslaved man who escaped to Canada and founded the Dawn Settlement.

memes.label: Journey to Kentucky
memes.description: Henson's leadership of eighteen enslaved people from Maryland to Kentucky in 1825, during which he refused an opportunity to escape to freedom in order to keep his word to his master

memes.label: Kentucky
memes.description: Kentucky, the slave state where Josiah Henson was sent in 1825 to manage the Amos Riley plantation in Davies County, and the place from which he escaped to freedom in 1830.

memes.label: Kindness and Cruelty
memes.description: The interplay of kindness and cruelty in Josiah Henson's narrative — how both enslaved and free people exercised moral agency within and against the institution of slavery.

memes.label: Labor and Economics of Slavery
memes.description: The labor systems, economic structures, and material conditions of slavery as depicted in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Lake Erie
memes.description: Great Lake forming the border between the United States and Canada, crossed by fugitive slaves including Josiah Henson and central to his settlement and rescue activities

memes.label: Lambeth
memes.description: The London palace of the Archbishop of Canterbury, where Josiah Henson was received for a memorable interview during his visit to England. (022-003)

memes.label: Lancaster
memes.description: Town in Ohio reached by Josiah Henson around three o'clock in the morning during the great Leonid meteor shower of November 1833, on a rescue mission to bring fugitive slaves from Kentucky to Canada.

memes.label: Legal Freedom Purchase
memes.description: Josiah Henson's attempt to legally purchase his freedom from Isaac Riley for $450, only to be betrayed when Riley sealed his manumission papers and instructed his brother Amos to demand an additional $650.

memes.label: Liverpool
memes.description: Major English port city from which Josiah Henson embarked for his return voyage to North America

memes.label: London
memes.description: London, where Josiah Henson exhibited black walnut lumber at the 1851 Great Exhibition, spoke to abolitionist audiences at Exeter Hall, and met with the Archbishop of Canterbury and Lord John Russell on his fundraising tours for the Dawn Settlement.

memes.label: Lord Grey
memes.description: A leading British statesman who proposed that Josiah Henson travel to India to superintend government cotton cultivation efforts on the American plan. (022-002)

memes.label: Lord John Russell
memes.description: British Prime Minister (1846–1852) who hosted Josiah Henson at a dinner at his Richmond Park estate during Henson's England tour. (022-005)(023-001)

memes.label: Dinner with Lord John Russell
memes.description: Josiah Henson's extraordinary day in June 1852 at the country estate of British Prime Minister Lord John Russell, where three hundred Sabbath School teachers — and one fugitive slave — were seated at the head of the table in a dining hall of state.

memes.label: Louisville
memes.description: Louisville, Kentucky, the Ohio River port where Josiah Henson disembarked in 1829 on his return from Maryland, and the place where his manumission papers were last seen before they were withheld by Amos Riley.

memes.label: Maimed for Life
memes.description: The 18-year-old Josiah Henson's permanent shoulder injury — both shoulder blades broken by overseer Bryce Litton while defending his master Amos Riley in a tavern brawl.

memes.label: Manual Labor School
memes.description: The vocational education initiative founded by Josiah Henson at the Dawn Settlement in Canada to teach practical skills to the Black community

memes.label: Manual Labor School Association
memes.description: The association that established and operated the manual-labor school at the Dawn Settlement in Canada, providing education in academic subjects and mechanical trades to Black children, white children, and Indigenous youth.

memes.label: Manumission
memes.description: The legal process by which enslaved people could be freed, a central theme in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Maryland
memes.description: Maryland, the border slave state where Josiah Henson was born in 1789 and spent his early life before removal to Kentucky.

memes.label: Maysville
memes.description: River town on the Ohio River in Kentucky, key transit point on Josiah Henson's escape route and site of his fugitive slave confrontation

memes.label: Meeting with John Rolfe
memes.description: Public mass meeting at Dawn Settlement where Rev. John Rolfe of Toronto presided over an inquiry into accusations against Henson, resulting in his full exoneration

memes.label: Methodism
memes.description: The Methodist religious tradition that shaped Josiah Henson's life as a preacher and community leader

memes.label: Methodist Circuit Preaching
memes.description: The system of Methodist circuit preaching in the early 19th-century American frontier, through which Josiah Henson became a licensed preacher and traveled between towns delivering sermons, a practice central to his religious identity and community standing.

memes.label: Methodist Episcopal Church
memes.description: The Methodist Episcopal Church, particularly its Ohio Conference, which provided Josiah Henson's religious conversion, preaching credentials, and the network connections that enabled his escape from slavery.

memes.label: Mississippi River
memes.description: The Mississippi River, down which Josiah Henson was taken in 1828 on a flatboat voyage to be sold in New Orleans, a journey that became the darkest spiritual crisis of his life and the prelude to his providential deliverance.

memes.label: Montgomery County
memes.description: Montgomery County, Maryland, the location of Isaac Riley's plantation where Josiah Henson spent his youth and early adulthood as an enslaved blacksmith and plantation superintendent.

memes.label: Motherhood under Slavery
memes.description: The experience and significance of motherhood for enslaved women in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Mr. Hibbard
memes.description: Canadian farmer and Josiah Henson's first employer after escaping slavery, who provided the family with shelter, work, and education.

memes.label: Mrs. Riley
memes.description: Wife of Isaac Riley, the Maryland slaveholder who owned Josiah Henson; she brought her own slaves to the Riley plantation and interacted with Henson during his return to Maryland.

memes.label: New England
memes.description: Region in the northeastern United States that Josiah Henson visited on his journey of observation, comparing its agricultural conditions to those of Canada.

memes.label: New Orleans
memes.description: New Orleans, the destination of the 1828 flatboat voyage on which Josiah Henson was to be sold as a slave, and the place where his young master Amos Riley fell deathly ill with river fever, reversing their fortunes and sparing Henson from sale into the Deep South.

memes.label: New York
memes.description: New York, the free state through which Josiah Henson traveled on Underground Railroad missions and fundraising journeys, and where he found key allies including James C. Fuller of Skeneateles and established commercial connections for the Dawn Settlement's lumber trade.

memes.label: Ohio
memes.description: The free state of Ohio, traversed repeatedly by Josiah Henson as a preacher, a fugitive, and a conductor on the Underground Railroad, serving as the threshold between slave Kentucky and freedom in Canada.

memes.label: Ohio Conference of the Methodist Episcopal Church
memes.description: The Ohio Conference of the Methodist Episcopal Church, whose annual session in Chillicothe was attended by Josiah Henson during his early ministry as a traveling exhorter and preacher.

memes.label: Ohio River
memes.description: The Ohio River, the great waterway separating slave states from free states, which Josiah Henson crossed multiple times — both as an enslaved driver of human property and as a conductor on the Underground Railroad.

memes.label: Overseer System
memes.description: The system of plantation management through overseers, a brutal institution documented extensively in Henson's narrative

memes.label: Pennsylvania
memes.description: A free state in the northern United States through which Josiah Henson traveled while conducting fugitive slaves to Canada via the Underground Railroad. (016-003)

memes.label: Plantation System
memes.description: The plantation system — the agricultural, economic, and social structure built on enslaved labor in the antebellum American South — as experienced and described by Josiah Henson.

memes.label: Portsmouth
memes.description: Ohio River town where Josiah Henson narrowly avoided capture during his escape from slavery

memes.label: Prayer
memes.description: The practice of prayer — personal and communal — as a central element of Josiah Henson's religious life, spiritual development, and public ministry.

memes.label: Preaching
memes.description: The practice of preaching and exhortation in the religious life of Josiah Henson and the enslaved community

memes.label: Ohio Preaching Tour
memes.description: Josiah Henson's preaching tour through Ohio and Kentucky in 1828 — a transformative period of independence, self-discovery, and spiritual leadership that gave him his first taste of respect as a free man and introduced him to the abolitionist network.

memes.label: Divine Providence
memes.description: The belief in God's guiding hand and intervention in human affairs, a recurring interpretive framework throughout Henson's narrative

memes.label: Providential Deliverance
memes.description: Henson's narrow escape from being sold into the Deep South at New Orleans — a turning point in his narrative where divine providence, operating through a chance encounter with a Methodist friend, saved him from permanent separation from his family.

memes.label: Publishing the Autobiography
memes.description: The publication of Josiah Henson's autobiography in London in 1858, with a 2,000-copy first edition — a strategic achievement that combined Henson's antislavery advocacy with his fundraising mission for the Dawn Settlement.

memes.label: Quakerism
memes.description: The Religious Society of Friends (Quakers), whose members provided critical aid to Henson and other freedom-seekers on the Underground Railroad

memes.label: Quaker Assistance
memes.description: The crucial assistance provided by Quaker communities to Josiah Henson and other fugitive slaves during their journeys north — including shelter at Richmond, Indiana, and a Quaker wagon driver who turned back from market to rescue a dying fugitive.

memes.label: Visits to the Ragged Schools
memes.description: Josiah Henson's engagement with the London Ragged School movement during his 1851–1852 visit to England, including speeches at Exeter Hall and advocacy for the education of poor children — efforts that drew the attention of British clergy, nobility, and the Prime Minister.

memes.label: Reading and Literacy
memes.description: The role of literacy and education in the lives of enslaved and free Black people, as depicted in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: The role of religion — particularly evangelical Christianity — in Josiah Henson's life, from his mother's early teachings through his conversion, preaching ministry, and community leadership.

memes.label: Return to Maryland
memes.description: Josiah Henson's return to Maryland to purchase his freedom, only to be cheated by his master's brother-in-law, Mathias, who stole his free papers and attempted to sell him.

memes.label: Rev. Ephraim Peabody
memes.description: Boston clergyman who introduced Josiah Henson to Samuel Eliot and the network of Boston philanthropists that funded the Dawn Settlement.

memes.label: Reverend William Brock
memes.description: London clergyman who opened his pulpit to Josiah Henson during his English fundraising tours and spoke at a celebratory event honoring Henson's work.

memes.label: Richmond, Indiana
memes.description: Quaker settlement in Indiana that provided aid to Josiah Henson and his party of fugitive slaves during a rescue journey to Canada

memes.label: Richmond Park
memes.description: Country estate of Lord John Russell in London, where Josiah Henson attended a notable dinner with Sabbath School teachers during his 1851–1852 England tour

memes.label: Sabbath
memes.description: The Sabbath — the day of rest and worship — as observed and experienced by Josiah Henson throughout his life, from slavery through freedom in Canada.

memes.label: Samuel Eliot
memes.description: Boston attorney who examined Josiah Henson's representations and drew up the proposal that secured funding for the Dawn Settlement sawmill enterprise.

memes.label: Samuel Gurney
memes.description: British banker, philanthropist, and abolitionist who introduced Josiah Henson to the Archbishop of Canterbury and served on Henson's support committee in London.

memes.label: Samuel Morley
memes.description: Congregational minister in London whose pulpit Josiah Henson preached from during his 1851 visit to England. (020-007)

memes.label: Sandusky
memes.description: City on Lake Erie in Ohio, key destination on the Underground Railroad and waypoint on Henson's escape route

memes.label: Scioto River
memes.description: River in Ohio encountered by Josiah Henson and his family during their escape journey, where they found General Hull's military road leading northward

memes.label: Second Journey on the Underground Railroad
memes.description: Josiah Henson's second expedition conducting fugitive slaves from Kentucky to Canada via the Underground Railroad, a dangerous journey recounted in Chapter 17 of his autobiography. (017-001)

memes.label: Sin and Redemption
memes.description: The themes of sin, redemption, and religious conversion in Josiah Henson's narrative, reflecting the central role of Christian theology in the experience of enslaved people

memes.label: Singing and Religious Music
memes.description: The role of singing, hymnody, and religious music in the worship and communal life of enslaved people, as depicted in Josiah Henson's narrative

memes.label: Sir Samuel Morton Peto
memes.description: British industrialist, Member of Parliament, and abolitionist who spoke at Josiah Henson's farewell event in London and supported the cause of the Canadian fugitive slave settlements.

memes.label: Slave Auction
memes.description: Josiah Henson and his family sold at auction following the death of Dr. McPherson, resulting in the permanent separation of Henson from his mother and siblings.

memes.label: Slave Quarters and Cabins
memes.description: The slave cabin — the dwelling place of the enslaved — as a site of family life, vulnerability, and the persistent threat of sale in Josiah Henson's narrative.

memes.label: Slavery
memes.description: The institution of chattel slavery in the United States as experienced first-hand by Josiah Henson, documented in his 1858 narrative from birth through escape and abolitionist advocacy.

memes.label: Speaking in England
memes.description: Henson's public speaking and preaching engagements throughout England during his 1851 visit, including appearances at Ragged Schools, meetings with the Archbishop of Canterbury, and fundraising for the Dawn Settlement

relations.label: mass meeting presided over by Rev. John Rolfe

memes.label: Thomas Binney
memes.description: London minister and abolitionist who hosted Josiah Henson's preaching engagements and served on the oversight committee for his fundraising during the 1851 England visit.

memes.label: Tobacco Cultivation
memes.description: Tobacco cultivation — the primary cash crop of the Upper South plantations where Josiah Henson was enslaved — and its role in shaping the labor, economy, and daily life described in his narrative.

memes.label: Toledo
memes.description: Port town on the southwestern shore of Lake Erie in Ohio, where Josiah Henson and a party of about thirty fugitive slaves took passage for Canada after a two-week journey through the wilderness.

memes.label: Toronto
memes.description: Major city in Upper Canada, home of Rev. John Rolfe who presided over the investigation into Josiah Henson's character and the Dawn Settlement

memes.label: Tour of Exploration
memes.description: Josiah Henson's exploratory journey on foot across Upper Canada in 1834 to find suitable land for a Black settlement, which led to the founding of the Dawn Settlement.

memes.label: Trust and Betrayal
memes.description: The moral economy of trust and betrayal in Josiah Henson's narrative — how fidelity was demanded of the enslaved while consistently betrayed by the enslaver.

memes.label: Underground Railroad
memes.description: The clandestine network of routes, safe houses, and individuals that assisted enslaved African Americans in escaping to free states and Canada — central to Henson's life as both fugitive and conductor.

relations.label: Second Journey on the Underground Railroad

memes.label: United States of America
memes.description: The nation where Josiah Henson was enslaved and from which he escaped; contrasted throughout the narrative with Canada and England as a land of oppression and legalized injustice.

memes.label: Vicksburg
memes.description: Vicksburg, the Mississippi River port where Josiah Henson stopped during his 1829 journey downriver to New Orleans and visited his former companions from Kentucky, witnessing firsthand the devastation of the Deep South slave system.

memes.label: Visit to England
memes.description: Josiah Henson's 1851 fundraising trip to England, where he exhibited black walnut boards at the World's Fair, met with British abolitionists, and raised funds to clear the debt of the Dawn Settlement's Manual Labor School.

memes.label: Washington
memes.description: Washington, the federal capital where Henson sold his master's plantation produce at market, where Francis "Master Frank" Riley established his business, and where the Riley family's financial affairs intersected with Henson's quest for freedom.

memes.label: Wheeling
memes.description: Wheeling, the Ohio River town in present-day West Virginia where Josiah Henson sold the wagon and purchased a boat to continue the 1825 journey to Kentucky, and the port he later returned to when traveling on foot after purchasing his freedom in Maryland.

memes.label: The World's Fair in London
memes.description: Josiah Henson's participation in the 1851 Great Exhibition in London, where he exhibited black walnut boards from the Dawn Settlement and received attention from Queen Victoria and other dignitaries.
